mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and generating negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in >58% of cases, or the regime-specific coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, extreme monetary accommodation reduces borrowing costs across the economy, stimulating investment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a quantile regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while consumer price inflation decelerates indicates a supply-side productivity boom, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL divergence signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction term is insignificant in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and inventory restocking, which triggers short-covering rallies and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery periods fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a hit rate above 50%, or the regime interaction term is statistically insignificant.
